data.title: Affirm and KAYAK announce the expansion of exclusive partnership into Canada
data.description:
• Affirm (NASDAQ:AFRM) and travel search engine KAYAK announced the expansion of their exclusive partnership into Canada. 
  • By selecting Affirm at checkout on ca.KAYAK.com, approved KAYAK travellers in Canada can split the total cost of flights, accommodations, and car rentals/sharing into monthly payments.
  • Consumers go through a quick, real-time eligibility check. If approved, they can choose the customized payment plan that best suits their needs and never pay any late or hidden fees.
  • AFRM shares up 1% on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Pagaya launches asset-backed revolving securitization program for point-of-sale financing
data.description:
Pagaya Technologies (NASDAQ:PGY) announced on Thursday the launch of an asset-backed revolving securitization program focused on point-of-sale financing, Pagaya Point of Sale Holdings Trust.

The technology provider for the financial ecosystem said POSH enables lenders to approve more customers at the point of sale.

The product is optimized for loans with shorter durations – typically six months – and credit profiles in the 600+ range.

The inaugural $300M transaction, POSH 2025-1, involves an 18-month revolving period, and is expected to close next week.

data.title: Platinum may emerge as an upside leader among precious metals – Fairlead Strategies
data.description:
Platinum (NYSEARCA:PPLT), (PLTM) may offer a compelling hedge opportunity, potentially emerging as an upside leader among precious metals (GLTR) as gold’s (GLD), (IAU) recent uptrend has paused, said Katie Stockton, founder of Fairlead Strategies.

During a CNBC interview, Stockton said that platinum “appears to be breaking out from a very long term, a multi-year triangle formation.”

This pattern is considered high probability for follow-through once the breakout occurs, suggesting potential for significant upward movement.

The abrdn Physical Platinum Shares ETF (NYSEARCA:PPLT) is up 18.2% year-to-date, and up 8.9% just in the past five days. Similarly, the GraniteShares Platinum Trust ETF (PLTM) is up 19% year-to-date and up 10% from five days ago.

Given ongoing volatility and a cautious view on equities, precious metals like platinum can serve as diversification assets, Stockton said. She indicated that sourcing exposure to other asset classes, particularly those showing promising technical setups, is prudent in the current environment.

Looking at the broader equity market, the founder observed a short-term loss of momentum in the S&P 500 (SP500), leading to a neutral bias. The longer-term outlook remains bearish based on deteriorating monthly indicators, prompting the view that investors feel like they want to “sell into the strength.”

While recommending a cautious approach to broad equity exposure, she advised maintaining long-term core positions in defensive sectors like consumer staples (XLP) and healthcare (XLV).

data.title: Bayer A.G.'s eye disease treatment gets approval in China
data.description:
• Bayer A.G. (OTCPK:BAYZF) said on Thursday that China’s National Medical Products Administration has approved Eylea 8 mg for age-related macular degeneration treatment.
  • The Eylea 8 mg is being jointly developed by Bayer and Regeneron (NASDAQ:REGN).
  • The approval is  based on positive results from the late-stage trial at week 48, which demonstrated comparable efficacy and safety of Eylea 8 mg with treatment intervals of 3 or 4 months.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Thursday: NKE, RL, INTU and WDAY
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures on Thursday were little changed, as market participants kept their focus on the passing of a major tax bill. Here are some stocks to watch on Thursday: 

  • Shares of Nike (NYSE:NKE) were +1.6% in pre-market trading. According to media reports, the Dow 30 component is planning to sell its products directly on Amazon (AMZN) in the U.S. for the first time since 2019. The world's largest shoe company has been struggling with falling sales and lower demand and has unveiled a turnaround plan, and the return to the e-commerce retailer's website is part of that strategy. Nike's (NYSE:NKE) products are currently sold by independent merchants on Amazon (AMZN).
  • Ralph Lauren (NYSE:RL) stock was -4% ahead of the opening bell. The iconic fashion firm delivered a FQ4 2025 top- and bottom-line beat. Ralph Lauren's (NYSE:RL) quarterly global direct-to-consumer comparable store sales climbed 13%, and 10% for the full fiscal year 2025. For fiscal 2026, the company sees revenue growth of low-single digits on a constant currency basis, "reflecting caution on the global operating environment."
  • Shares of Intuit (NASDAQ:INTU) will get some attention, ahead of the financial and accounting software developer's FQ3 2025 results after the closing bell. Wall Street expects the Mountain View, Calif.-based firm to earn $10.91 per share on revenue of $7.56B. Intuit (NASDAQ:INTU) owns several software products such as tax preparation tool TurboTax, bookkeeper QuickBooks, and online credit score service Credit Karma.
  • Workday (NASDAQ:WDAY) stock will also get some eyes, ahead of the enterprise management cloud software firm's FQ1 2026 report after hours. Wall Street expects Pleasanton, Calif.-based WDAY to earn $2.01 per share on revenue of $2.22B. The top-line figure is seen rising more than 15% Y/Y, while the bottom-line number is anticipated to grow nearly 12%. Workday (NASDAQ:WDAY) provides a platform that helps organizations manage money, people and agents.

data.title: J Capital says it's short Marqeta
data.description:
• J Capital Research said on Thursday it's short the card issuing platform Marqeta (NASDAQ:MQ).
  • "An FBI investigation could prompt MQ to lose up to 70% of its business. Future revenue looks weak," said J Capital Research in a report.
  • MQ shares were trading -5.37% pre-market at $4.93.

data.title: CBAK Energy Technology announces $20M share buyback program
data.description:
• CBAK Energy Technology (NASDAQ:CBAT) approved a share repurchase program with authorization to purchase up to $20M of shares of its common stock. 
  • The share repurchase program is designed to regain compliance with Nasdaq’s minimum bid price requirement. 
  • This program terminates on May 20, 2026.
  • CBAT shares fall 4% premarket on Thursady. 
  • Source: Press release
